faqs.question: What does a whole-house water filtration system do?
faqs.answer: A whole-house system filters all the water entering your home at the main supply line. This means every tap, shower, and appliance gets clean, filtered water — removing sediment, chlorine, heavy metals, and other contaminants.

faqs.question: How often do whole-house filters need to be replaced?
faqs.answer: Most whole-house filters need replacement every 3–6 months for sediment pre-filters and every 5–10 years for the main filtration media, depending on your water quality and usage. We offer maintenance plans to keep your system running optimally.

faqs.question: Is a whole-house filtration system worth the investment?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. It protects your plumbing and appliances from sediment and mineral buildup, improves the taste and smell of your water, and provides healthier water for drinking, cooking, and bathing throughout your entire home.
